Pub. L. 104–287This amends 49:70117(b)(2) by updating a cross-reference. Section 4 of the Land Remote Sensing Policy Act of 1992 (, ) repealed the Land Remote-Sensing Commercialization Act of 1984 ( et seq.). The substantive provisions of the Land Remote Sensing Policy Act of 1992, which replaced the Land Remote-Sensing Commercialization Act of 1984, were classified to the United States Code at et seq.Public Law 102–555106 Stat. 416615 U.S.C. 420115 U.S.C. 5601

children.heading: .—Executive Agencies
children.label: a .—Executive Agencies
children.text: Except as provided in this chapter, a person is not required to obtain from an executive agency a license, approval, waiver, or exemption to launch a launch vehicle or operate a launch site or reentry site, or to reenter a reentry vehicle.

children.heading: .—Federal Communications Commission and Secretary of Commerce
children.label: b .—Federal Communications Commission and Secretary of Commerce

children.text: the Federal Communications Commission under the Communications Act of 1934 ( et seq.); or47 U.S.C. 151
the Secretary of Commerce under chapter 601 of this title.
may not adopt or have in effect a law, regulation, standard, or order inconsistent with this chapter; but

may adopt or have in effect a law, regulation, standard, or order consistent with this chapter that is in addition to or more stringent than a requirement of, or regulation prescribed under, this chapter.

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: d .—Consultation
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation is encouraged to consult with a State to simplify and expedite the approval of a space launch or reentry activity.

carry out this chapter consistent with an obligation the United States Government assumes in a treaty, convention, or agreement in force between the Government and the government of a foreign country; and
consider applicable laws and requirements of a foreign country when carrying out this chapter.

children.heading: .—Launch Not an Export; Reentry Not an Import
children.label: f .—Launch Not an Export; Reentry Not an Import
children.text: A launch vehicle, reentry vehicle, or payload that is launched or reentered is not, because of the launch or reentry, an export or import, respectively, for purposes of a law controlling exports or imports, except that payloads launched pursuant to foreign trade zone procedures as provided for under the Foreign Trade Zones Act () shall be considered exports with regard to customs entry.19 U.S.C. 81a–81u

children.label: A
children.text: a launch, reentry, operation of a launch vehicle or reentry vehicle, operation of a launch site or reentry site, or other space activity the Government carries out for the Government; or

children.label: B
children.text: planning or policies related to the launch, reentry, operation, or activity under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: A government astronaut being carried within a launch vehicle or reentry vehicle under this chapter.

children.label: B
children.text: A government astronaut performing activities directly relating to the launch, reentry, or other operation of the launch vehicle or reentry vehicle under this chapter.
